summary: The plan is structurally sound and correctly identifies the composition of existing shaderValidation and geometryValidator modules into a unified gate. Several adjustments are needed based on actual codebase state.

notes: REQUIRED_ATTRIBUTES is not a single central constant — each geometry system (pointCloud, particleField, ribbonField) defines its own REQUIRED_ATTRIBUTES locally. The health gate must either: (a) import each system's constant, or (b) have each system expose a validate() method or its required attributes via the GeometrySystem interface.

Geometry validation currently runs inside each system's init() — after initAllForValidation() all systems are initialized, so their BufferGeometry instances exist and can be validated externally. The gate should iterate ModeManager's internal systems to access their meshes' geometries.

The function signature `runStartupHealthGate(renderer, scene, camera, geometrySystems)` should accept the ModeManager (or an array of { name, geometry, requiredAttributes } tuples) rather than raw geometrySystems, since ModeManager wraps the systems.
ShaderErrorCollector is created in scene.ts and passed via LoopDeps — the health gate function needs it as a parameter too.
Correctly identifies that the current validateShaderCompilation() throws (renderLoop.ts ~line 173) and that the health gate should be non-throwing.
The existing code path at lines 166-184 in renderLoop.ts is the correct integration point.
The placeholder rotation loop (lines 207-210) already stays alive independently, so blocking steady-state naturally keeps the placeholder visible — no additional work needed for that.
types.ts already has AttributeSpec and GeometrySystem — adding HealthGateResult here is the right location.
Consider also adding a GeometryErrorDetail type with systemName to distinguish which of the three systems failed.
Mocking patterns are well-established in the existing test suite (vitest vi.fn(), vi.spyOn(), module mocks for three).
Follow existing test ID convention: use T-052-XX prefixes.
Prefer a new file (healthGate-integration.test.ts) over modifying scene-integration.test.ts to keep test scope clear.
The requestAnimationFrame mock pattern from existing tests (vi.spyOn with frame counter) is the right approach for verifying the loop stays on placeholder.

missingConsiderations: The plan does not address how to access individual geometry systems' BufferGeometry from outside the system. ModeManager's internal `systems` array holds GeometrySystem instances, but GeometrySystem interface (types.ts) does not expose the underlying mesh or geometry. Either: (a) add an optional `getGeometry?(): BufferGeometry | null` to GeometrySystem, or (b) have each system run its own validation in init() and expose a `validationResult` property, or (c) have ModeManager collect and expose validation results after initAllForValidation().
Each system's REQUIRED_ATTRIBUTES are module-private constants. The health gate either needs them exported, or needs a different approach to per-system validation (see above).
The plan should specify ordering: step 3 (types) must be implemented before step 1 (healthGate.ts) since the types are imported by the gate module.

riskAssessment.mitigation: Option (c) is lowest-friction: have ModeManager.initAllForValidation() collect GeometryValidationResult from each system and expose them. This avoids changing the GeometrySystem interface and keeps REQUIRED_ATTRIBUTES private to each system.

Wrap the existing validateShaderCompilation call in a try-catch within the health gate, or refactor it to return a result instead of throwing. The try-catch approach is simpler and doesn't break existing tests for shaderValidation.ts.
